rules.summary: Unless otherwise stated, this Part applies to a non-directive friendly society.
In this Part, the following definitions shall apply:

sections.chapter_title: Long-Term Insurance Business Margin of Solvency: Classes I and II

rules.summary: A firm must calculate the required margin of solvency in respect of classes I and II as the aggregate of the results arrived at by applying the calculation described in [2.2](/pra-rules/friendly

sections.chapter_title: Long-Term Insurance Business Margin of Solvency: Classes III and VII

rules.summary: A firm must calculate the required margin of solvency in respect of classes III and VII in accordance with [3.2](/pra-rules/friendly-society-required-margin#76cdb71191c94cc9a17fa66c02df1c0f "3.2
If and in so far as a firm bears an investment risk, the calculation in 2.2 must be applied.
If and in so far as a firm bears no investment risk and if the allocation to cover management expenses in the relevant contract:

sections.chapter_title: Long-Term Insurance Business Margin of Solvency: Class IV

rules.summary: A firm must calculate the required margin of solvency in respect of class IV by applying the calculation in [2.2](/pra-rules/friendly-society-required-margin#4deb39da00144e47b924d724ae81595d "2.

sections.chapter_title: Long-Term Insurance Business Margin of Solvency: Class V

rules.summary: A firm must calculate the required margin of solvency in respect of class V as 1% of the assets of the relevant tontine.

sections.chapter_title: Long-Term Insurance Business Margin of Solvency: Class VI

sections.chapter_title: General Insurance Business Solvency Margin: Claims Basis

rules.summary: This Chapter does not apply to a firm that has not been in existence long enough to acquire a reference period.
A firm must calculate a claims basis solvency margin by applying the calculation in 8.3 to 8.9.
If the provision for claims outstanding at:
A firm must divide the aggregate obtained under 8.3(1) or (2) by the number of months in the _reference period
